description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:stable image with the docker:stable-dind service and defines variables for PostgreSQL, Helm, and Kubernetes. Define stages: build, test, code_quality, sast, dependency_scanning, container_scanning, license_management, deploy, production. In the build job (stage build) run `registry_login` then `docker build --build-arg HTTP_PROXY="$HTTP_PROXY" --build-arg HTTPS_PROXY="$HTTPS_PROXY" -t "$CI_APPLICATION_REPOSITORY:$CI_APPLICATION_TAG" .` and push the image. In the test job (stage test) run `setup_test_db` to export DATABASE_URL and then execute the project’s test suite (e.g., `pytest`). In the code_quality, sast, dependency_scanning, and container_scanning jobs (stage test) run the respective functions `code_quality`, `sast`, `dependency_scanning`, and `container_scanning`, each of which invokes a Docker container with the appropriate security‑product image and scans the code or image. In the license_management job (stage test) run `license_management` which executes `/run.sh analyze .`. In the deploy job (stage deploy) run `install_dependencies`, `ensure_namespace`, `download_chart`, and then `deploy stable` to install the Helm chart with the built image, and in the production job (stage production) run `scale stable` to adjust replica counts.
